reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. "Infinitely many" is expressed correctly as ∀ N, ∃ n > N, which is the standard equivalent. Using n : ℕ instead of ℤ is equivalent because any integer expressible as a sum of two squares is necessarily ≥ 0, so the set of qualifying integers coincides with the set of qualifying naturals. Using i : Fin 6 → ℕ for the square bases is equivalent to allowing integers, since a² = |a|² means every sum of two integer squares equals a sum of two natural-number squares. The Fin 6 packaging just groups six existentially quantified naturals (two per term n, n+1, n+2). No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
